title: PROP-031 BAU 3b is over-escalating to pending-human: matrix step 1 invariant (major/critical → escalate) is wrong for BAU triage context. Need context-aware routing — severity isn't the escalation signal; operator-only-judgment is.
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-NNN). Operator reviews PROP before implementation. Tinker designs the matrix tightening; operator + tinker apply the prompt edits.
context: PROP-031 BAU 3b ran for the first time at 2026-05-12T05:12Z. It correctly applied the PROP-027 5-action routing matrix to status='open' items ≥12h old. Of the items it found, 7 were classified as major/critical and matrix step 1 invariant ('severity ∈ {major, critical} → ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. Invariant. Never auto-close major/critical.') fired on all 7. They all moved to status='pending-human'.

Operator review 2026-05-12T05:30Z: of the 7 escalated, ZERO genuinely require operator-only judgment. Breakdown: 2 critical citation failures (DOI/URL points to wrong paper — verification work for analyst), 2 major strawman/precision (analyst defense work), 2 major citation/precision (verification + narrow patch), 1 major verdict-reclassification proposal (analyst can draft EXP; operator only approves the verdict commit when EXP lands, not earlier).

Operator manually rerouted all 7 from pending-human to assigned-analyst with appropriate class_hints. Filed HNOTE-OPERATOR-CITATION-CLUSTER-WIN-013-014-SEC-6-14-001 for the 3 citation items as a batched verification EXP.

Operator framing 2026-05-12T05:32Z: 'the two criticals don't need human, they just need the citation fixed.' Implicit: severity tier is not a reliable proxy for 'operator-only judgment required.' Critical citation fixes are analyst-class work; critical verdict commits on already-published material ARE operator-only.

Counter-context: matrix step 1's conservative posture was correct for the M1 stale-sweep introduction (PROP-026, where the safety net was new and over-escalation was the safe default while the system learned). PROP-031's BAU 3b is the primary throughput path — it operates on a different item population (recently-raised curmudgeon items, not weeks-old stale residue) and should route by what-the-work-actually-needs, not by severity tier.
operator_intent: Tighten the routing matrix's escalate-to-human criteria to fire on actual operator-only signals, not severity proxy. Distinguish BAU 3b's invocation context from M1's. Avoid creating new failure modes (silent auto-close of genuinely-load-bearing major/critical work).

tasks.description: Enumerate the actual categories that require operator-only judgment, independent of severity tier. Goal: a small concrete list that the matrix can check, not a vague 'use your judgment' rule.
candidate_criteria: Verdict commit on already-published WIN where verdict_history shows operator authored the most recent change (operator-discretion preserved by PROP-028's verdict_history invariant).
Modification to a NEVER_PUSH file (test.js, build.js, CLAUDE.md, build-scripts/*, monitor/prompts/*.md) — operator-applied per CLAUDE.md File Ownership.
Legal/strategic/external-communication content (Timestamp Error tab framing, public-facing scoreboard, FAQ).
Physical-world verification (e.g., a fact-check requiring operator to consult external instrument data, dome-author DM context, or operator-side knowledge not in the workspace).
Pipeline-config change (decider-mode.json flip, scheduled task cadence change, model assignment, etc.).
explicitly_NOT_operator_only: Citation precision (DOI/URL wrong, paper resolves elsewhere) — analyst verifies and patches. Severity irrelevant.
Strawman defense (curmudgeon raised; analyst writes counter-defense). Severity irrelevant.
Narrow-precision wording fix (single-line or single-paragraph). Severity irrelevant.
Verdict-change PROPOSAL drafting (analyst writes the EXP; operator only weighs in at integration time).

tasks.description: Replace the blanket 'major/critical → escalate' with context-aware step 1.

proposed_matrix_revision.current_step_1: severity ∈ {major, critical} → ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. Invariant. Never auto-close major/critical.

proposed_step_1.M1_stale_sweep_context: severity ∈ {major, critical} AND age ≥ N_DAYS → ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. (Original conservative invariant; preserved for M1 because items reaching M1 by definition survived all earlier triage and might be operator-territory.)
proposed_step_1.M3_carry_over_context: severity ∈ {major, critical} AND no prior route action this cycle → ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. (Same conservative posture; preserved.)
proposed_step_1.BAU_3b_context: Item description matches one of the operator-only-judgment categories from task_1 → ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN, regardless of severity. Otherwise route per remaining matrix (steps 2-6: WONTFIX / PATCH / NARROW-PATCH / ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON / ROUTE-TO-ANALYST). Severity tier informs class_hint but NOT escalate-vs-route decision.

tasks.deliverable: Single canonical statement of step 1 with three invocation-context branches in monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md.

tasks.description: BAU 3b's escalate check shouldn't be a magic regex — it's an LLM judgment call ('does this description require operator-only knowledge?'). Specify the prompt-side criteria so decider's LLM applies it consistently.
tasks.proposed_check: When BAU 3b walks an item, ask: 'To resolve this issue, does the decider need information that ONLY the operator has, OR is the work the operator can decline-only (verdict commit on published material, etc.)?' If yes → escalate. If no → route per matrix steps 2-6 by description shape. Specifically, items resolvable by 'fetch a URL, verify cite, patch wins.json' are NEVER operator-only regardless of severity.
examples_to_include_in_prompt: ISS-1944 (Van Camp DOI 404 on WIN-013): operator-only? NO — fetch correct DOI, patch. Route to analyst class_hint='verification'.
ISS-1921 (WIN-034 SC reclassification proposal): operator-only? NO at drafting stage — analyst writes the EXP. Operator weighs in at verdict commit. Route to analyst class_hint='holistic'.
Example operator-only: 'Operator should decide whether to publish the dome-author DM contact as on-site evidence.' — Yes, operator-only. Escalate.
Example operator-only: 'test.js needs new assertion for verdict_history invariant.' — Yes, NEVER_PUSH file. Escalate.
Example operator-only: 'Schedule-cadence flip from BAU 21d to burndown 7d.' — Yes, pipeline-config. Escalate.

tasks.description: Re-run the 2026-05-12T05:12Z BAU 3b decisions under the proposed matrix. The 7 items operator manually rerouted should have routed correctly without manual intervention.
tasks.expected_outcome: All 7 items (ISS-1921, 1933, 1944, 1945, 1948, 1957, 1958) route to analyst with appropriate class_hints under the new matrix. Zero escalate-to-human on this batch. Tinker confirms the design would have produced the same routing operator applied manually.

tasks.description: Don't over-correct. The conservative posture exists for good reason (M1 catches genuinely-stuck items, M3 catches things curmudgeon already flagged as carry-over). Tinker should preserve those branches and only relax BAU 3b. Also don't add complex severity-AND-category logic — keep step 1 single-statement-per-context.
tasks.deliverable: Explicit 'do not touch' section in PROP: M1 step 1 invariant preserved; M3 step 1 invariant preserved; only BAU 3b step 1 is revised.

constraints: PROP-NNN authoring authority only. Operator reviews PROP before implementation.
Output one PROP under monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-bau-triage-severity-vs-judgment.json or similar.
Respect PROP-027 (the matrix) + PROP-031 (BAU 3b). This is a tightening of PROP-031's invocation of step 1, not a redesign of either upstream PROP.
related_directives: DIRECTIVE-20260511-001-decider-throughput-audit-and-tap-out-investigation.json (PROP-031 — applied the matrix to BAU 3b, surfacing the over-escalation pattern)
operator_notes_to_tinker: The empirical 7-item batch from 2026-05-12T05:12Z is the canonical test case. If your proposed matrix doesn't route all 7 correctly (per task_4), the design isn't ready.

The Sonnet-decider context matters: Sonnet is more compliant with explicit prompt rules than Opus' judgment-heavy style. A simple 'check these 5 criteria' enumeration will work better than 'use judgment.' Lean toward concrete enumeration.

If after honest design you find the conservative-default-escalate posture is actually right and the 7 items today were edge cases the matrix should have escalated correctly... say so. Operator wants the right answer.
